publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-03-02 23:59:09 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, Legal Documents, Newspapers, Photographs, Printed Materials
did.abstract: Papers of Josiah Butler Williams, banker, businessman, and New York State Senator, contain business, political, and personal papers, accounts, legal documents and correspondence, 1809-1883. Subjects include banking, canals, the lumber business, railroads, and real estate; also, Cornell University, Ovid Academy, the New York and Erie Telegraph Company, the Ithaca and Owego Railroad Company, the Ithaca Cotton Mills, the Merchants and Farmers' Bank, Ithaca, and the Sodus Canal.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was reprocessed in 2025 and a new finding aid created. Includes former collections 422, 540, 1023, and 2550.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Williams family papers, #1148. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers of Josiah Butler Williams, banker, businessman, and New York State Senator, contain business, political, and personal papers, accounts, legal documents and correspondence, 1809-1883. Subjects include banking, canals, the lumber business, railroads, and real estate; also, Cornell University, Ovid Academy, the New York and Erie Telegraph Company, the Ithaca and Owego Railroad Company, the Ithaca Cotton Mills, the Merchants and Farmers' Bank, Ithaca, and the Sodus Canal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers of Mary Hardy Williams, wife of Josiah B., contain personal correspondence, a diary, bills of H. S. Williams (1900-1911) social notes, clippings, and family accounts, 1824-1905.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers of Augusta H. Williams, daughter of Josiah B., contain personal correspondence, accounts, and business papers, 1879-1948.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Papers of Henry Shaler Williams, son of Josiah B., geologist, containing lecture and research notes used at Cornell and Yale Universities; professional correspondence and scrapbooks of photographs and clippings; articles and manuscripts for publication; photographs, maps, and plates; and miscellaneous printed matter, ca. 1864-1917. Also includes H. S. Williams' personal and household bills (1900-1911). Subjects include geology and politics. Includes Ithaca to Owego geological observations, July 21, 1885, and miscellaneous H. S. Williams items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The collection also includes miscellaneous papers of Charlotte, Jane, Ella, and Otis Williams and Louisa, Jane, and Charles Elias Hardy, and contains photographs of the Williams family as well as genealogical information. Plans by William Henry Miller for the Williams residence.

controlaccess.subject: Politicians.
Geologists.
College teachers
Bankers.
Women -- Diaries.
Telegraph.
Real property, Exchange of.
Railroads.
Politics, Practical
Lumber trade
Paleontology -- Study and teaching.
Geology -- Study and teaching.
Cotton manufacture
Canals.
Banks and banking
controlaccess.corpname: Ithaca and Owego Rail Road Company
New York and Erie Telegraph Company
Sigma Xi, the Scientific Research Society

did.unittitle: Series I. Josiah Butler Williams papers
Subseries Ia. Personal papers and correspondence
Letters to Josiah B. Williams from his children
Letters from Josiah B. Williams to his children
Letters from Josiah B. Williams to his brothers
Letters to Josiah B. Williams from his children
Letters from Mary Hardy Williams and Walter Williams
Christmas poem from Francis M. Finch to Josiah Williams
Subseries Ib. Business papers and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many business papers relate to the Williams Brothers and Shaw.

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Michigan lands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Henry Darling, Iona, Michigan (1855); articles of agreement with Henry Darling, Elihu Ring (1856); cost and taxes of Michigan lands; four township maps.

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Michigan lands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence concerning Davenport lands.

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Michigan lands
Bounty land deeds from War of 1812, Michigan lands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by President James Buchanan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes receipts from Delaware Lackawanna and Western to Mrs. Josiah Butler Williams, 1891.

did.unittitle: Business papers of Josiah B. Williams, Timothy S. Williams, Charles E. Hardy, and J.E. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 10 shares of stock in Ithaca Cotton Mills (1833)

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Chicago lumber
Business papers relating to Ithaca Cotton Mills of Timothy S. Williams and Josiah B. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes inventory of merchandise (1838)

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Illinois lands of Timothy S. and Josiah B. Williams
Bounty Land deeds authorized by Acts of 1820 and 1847 Illinois lands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signed by President James Taylor.

did.unittitle: Political correspondence of Josiah B. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes remarks on Canal question (1853); Diagram of New York Senate Chamber (1865); and letter from Millard Fillmore (1849).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Cornell Trustee meeting announcements; Ithaca Academy directory (1865-1866); Charter of the Village of Ithaca (1851); By-Laws of Ithaca Savings Bank (1841); .

did.unittitle: Business papers relating to Anti-Slavery Convention

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains list of resolutions passed, delegates present, convention minutes, and documents announcing JB Williams as delegate. Also includes a Feb. 12, 1838 letter to Rev. John B. Shaw, in Attica, from William Lawrence Chaplin.

did.unittitle: Merchants and Farmers bank account statements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Watermen's Association constitution; letter from Alvan Steward inviting Williams to the New York State Anti-Slavery Society (1841)

did.unittitle: Ithaca and Owego Rail Road papers
Ithaca and Owego Rail Road papers
Cayuga Susquehana Rail Road Company ledger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and documents relating to the New York and Erie Telegraph Association and mentions of Ezra Cornell's leadership, as well as a letter from Ezra Cornell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Report of Commissioners relative to Draining Cayuga Marshes" and notes from the commissioners. Also included is a printed pamphlet describing the Academy at Ovid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed Senate Acts and information on canal and railroad construction and petitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes printed circulars and articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Ezra Cornell about passage of a law enabling organization of a company for importation and improvement of livestock; material about draining of Cayuga marshes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes broadside advertising real estate sale and properties in Ithaca, New York (February 17, 1860).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters from the Board of Trustees of the New York State Agriculture College and Ezra Cornell's donation for the building of Cornell University (September, 1864).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Josiah Williams recounting the Battle of Nashville with General John Bell Hood's army in Tennessee (January 2, 1865); correspondence regarding the

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Elihu Burritt regarding "compensated Emancipation."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder five contains some personal correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes draft of act to aid in the establishment of the New York State Agricultural College in Ovid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes report of committee for building contractors, Agricultural College correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters from Ezra Cornell, one inviting Williams to a board of trustees meeting and one inviting him to the first commencement of Cornell University.

did.unittitle: New York State Agricultural College correspondence and related agricultural publications

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes small notebook of subscriptions in aid of the college and Tompkins County Horticultural Society booklet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Many items are addressed to Charles Hardy or George Williams, as part of the Merchant's and Farmer's bank

did.unittitle: Subseries Ic. Banking documents and accounts
Ledger and papers relating to the organization of the Merchants and Farmers National Bank of Ithaca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume.

did.unittitle: Articles of Association for the Merchants and Farmers Bank

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bound volume.

did.unittitle: Business papers related to Treasury Department
Bound volume of New York State Assembly Bills
Bound volume of New York State Assembly Bills
Bound volume of New York State Assembly Bills
Bound volume of New York State Senate Bills
Bound volume of New York State Senate Bills
Bound volume of New York State Senate Bills
Bound volume of New York State Senate Bills
Constitution of the State of New York
Printed pamphlets and publications related to banking and finance
Printed pamphlets and publications related to canals
New York State Senate floor plans
Series II. Mary Hardy Williams papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Miss Burr's school, Ithaca (1834); Ithaca Female Seminary (1836); and Ithaca Academy (1837-1841).

did.unittitle: Subseries IIb. Business papers and accounts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Kept by Mary Hardy Williams and daughters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from the Harriet Tubman home in Auburn, NY (1902); meeting minutes from the Free Kindergarten; and various local churches, hospitals, colleges, and missionary efforts.

did.unittitle: Business papers - homestead account, estate account
Incoming correspondence from Josiah B. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Malvina Higgins regarding the students of Maryville College (1878).

Letters from George Walker (uncle) and Annie (aunt)
Letters from Mary E. Hardy Ostrom (aunt)
Letters from Chauncey Pratt Williams and Martha Hough Williams
Letters from Chauncey Pratt Williams and Martha Hough Williams
Letters from Clara Williams Tanner from Europe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from John Tanner.

did.unittitle: Letters from children of George Russell Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters from Elizabeth Lee, Ruth Ellen Fitschen, and Elsie Robinson.

did.unittitle: Letters from children of Henry Shaler Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters from Clifford, Charlotte, Arthur Shaler, and Roger Henry.

did.unittitle: Letters from Roger B. Williams, Jr.
Letters from children of Jane Williams Newman, Mary Louise, Henry Otis, Charles Hardy, and Robert
Letters from children of Otis Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters from Katherine, Margaret, Dorothy, Laura.

did.unittitle: Letters from children of Otis Williams
Letters from Harriet St. John Simpson
Correspondence concerning Mrs. William H. Roanes, Magnolia, Mississippi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Isaiah Thompson was a black man employed by Mary H. Williams. These letters discuss his whereabouts, his requests for necessities, and letter from his son.

did.unittitle: Letter drafts by Mary H. Williams
Scriptures, miscellaneous writings of MHW, and addresses
Diary - regarding Pauline and death of Roger
Diary - trip to Utica with Jane L. Hardy
Diary - description of death of Charles E. Hardy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Ellen Brown (wife of Robert Brown) with a picture of their daughter, Nellie (1888).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from children

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings and correspondence regarding childrens hospital.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes articles of partnership between the Williams Brothers (1845.)

did.unittitle: Correspondence, notes, invitations, and calling cards
Correspondence, notes, invitations, and calling cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes creative writing exercises and school papers.

did.unittitle: Mary Hardy Williams writing exercises and correspondence
Mary Hardy Williams papers and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes diaries from 1834-1841, confession of love from J.B. Williams, and correspondence regarding death of son Charlie (1846).

did.unittitle: Mary Hardy Williams papers and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, clippings, a handmade prayer book from a church congregation, invitations, and note about her children's vaccination status.

did.unittitle: Williams family genealogy with letters to Augusta Hardy Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies of "Ancestry and Descendents of Josiah Williams, 1656-1910." One copy belonged to Augusta Hardy Williams and contains her notes and corrections.

did.unittitle: Williams family genealogy pamphlets, notes, and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copies, some annotated, of "Ancestry and Descendents of Josiah Williams, 1656-1910."

did.unittitle: Williams family genealogy publication correspondence with Augusta Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Hardy/Williams family documents from the 1700s.

did.unittitle: School records and medical notes for Mary Williams' children
Household notes, recipes, and family programs
Death notices and memorials for Williams family members
Division of property, likely Cliff Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an account of Mary Dennis's death, 1836.

did.unittitle: Business papers and letters to George Russell, Henry Shaler, Roger Butler, and Otis Lincoln Williams
Documents and correspondence relating to Judy Jackson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Judy Jackson (1814-1897) was a formerly enslaved woman employed by the Williams. Correspondence includes discussions of Jackson's death, her grave stone, her personal effects, and dictated letters to her relatives. Also includes letter from Malvina Higgins about Judy Jackson and describes Judy's history, including that her son was sold when he was eight.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes accounts of Louise Hardy, Mary Williams, and Jane Hardy with estate (1870-1882); Ella Williams account with Clara Williams Tanner; Diary telling of Louisa's death (1869); notebook of problems with rural churches; 1903 trip of Newman family to California, Oregon, and Canada; The Wellesley Prelude description of trip to Mexico by Laura Lyon Williams.

did.unittitle: Receipt book, Merchants' and Farmers' bank
Mortgage book, Merchants' and Farmers' bank
George R. Williams writings, journal, and school papers
George R. Williams writings, school papers, and obituary
Louisa Williams writings, compositions, and notes
Roger B. Williams writings and notes
Otis Williams drawings and practice books
Asa S. Fiske sermons and pamphlets
Photographs of Asa S. Fiske and family
Inventory of articles sent to Dewitt Historical Society and Home Economics collection
"The World's Columbian Exposition" issued by the Department of Photography
Edith Clifford Williams notebooks and notes
Book titled "Aloha Comes of Age", by Frances Coleman Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recounts the travels of Frances Williams on the boat "Aloha"

did.unittitle: Small leather and velvet wallet with boot-lacing keys
Flattened rubber goat with a "1901" tag and "keep your eyes open and your mouth shut" written on the sides
Leather badge with "A Brother is born for adversity: presented to Arthur the Fearless" written on it and a quarter attached to it
Subseries IIIc. Augusta H. Williams papers
Business papers and letters of Augusta Hardy Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some clippings and "The Transcript" from Vassar College.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes samples of black fabrics.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIId. Ella Williams - box 35 original order

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Beattie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents and correspondence about the Dorcas Society, as well as a hand colored photograph of unidentified East Asian women.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Wells College programs and printed material, and A.S. Fiske correspondence.

Letters to Ella Susan Williams from Mary Hardy Williams
Sympathy notes at death of Ella
Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from Mary Hardy Williams
Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from family
Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from Augusta Hardy Williams
Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from Ella Susan Williams

Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from children of Henry Shaler Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes many letters from Florence, Guayabal, Cuba.

Letters to Charlotte Elizabeth Williams from Otis L. Williams and family

scopecontent.p: Includes scripture lessons, draft letters, and sermons.

did.unittitle: Business papers of Charlotte Elizabeth Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes student papers and compositions.

did.unittitle: Letters to Clara Martha Williams from Mary Hardy Williams
Account books of Clara and John Tanner
"The Fence" publication from Hopkins Grammar School
"A Discourse…in consequence of the Death of the Hon. Timothy S. Williams"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes draft of Articles of Association for the Merchant's and Farmer's Bank, April 18, 1838.

did.unittitle: Business papers and personal correspondence of Joseph E. Shaw and Timothy S. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Hen ry W. Sage (1835) and undated diary of trip to Detroit, Ann Arbor, Kalamazoo, White River, and Niles.

did.unittitle: Business papers and personal correspondence of Joseph E. Shaw and Timothy S. Williams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes list of dry goods (1843).

did.unittitle: Business papers of Joseph E. Shaw

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes insurance policy (1852).

Joseph E. Shaw and Woodworth correspondence

Joseph E. Shaw and Woodworth correspondence

Joseph E. Shaw and Woodworth correspondence

Series V. Hardy and Walker family papers
Subseries Va. General Hardy family documents
Hardy and Walker family genealogy and estate documents
Correspondence between Gorham Co. and Augusta Hardy Williams concerning a memorial for Elias Hardy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1865 deed.

did.unittitle: Correspondence concerning the Hardy memorial in New Brunswick
"A Biographical Sketch of Elias Hardy" by Rev. W. O. Raymond
England: Hardy ancestral home sketches and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes, copy of will of Thomas Walker (1869), and appointment letter of William Walker to New York Infantry (1822)

did.unittitle: Catalog and Class list of Utica Female Seminary (Louisa W. Hardy)
Catalog of the Law School of the University of Albany
Miscellaneous notes about the Civil War
Papers from the Fourth Presbyterian Church, Albany
Letters and papers of Louisa W. Hardy (Daughter of Charles Hardy)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a large lock of Louisa's hair.

did.unittitle: Business papers of Charles E. Hardy
Letters to Charles Hardy and other family members

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings and thoughts regarding the Civil War, and letter from Charles' sister regarding the state of the government elections and race relations in the south (1867-1868).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some personal letters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Hardy's saw mill patents with illustration.

did.unittitle: Pages from an account book from Utica
Letters to Louisa Hardy Mrs. Charles Hardy)
Letters to Louisa and Charles Hardy from Jane Hardy
Scrapbook of clippings collected by Louisa Hardy and pasted in an 1816 atlas
Letters from Harriet N. Williams (daughter of Timothy Shaler)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters describing the reaction to President Abraham Lincoln's death, as well as recent events at Yale University.

did.unittitle: Manuscript papers, scripture lesson, prayers, and bills
Manuscript papers of Woman's Union Benevolent Society and Ithaca Ladies Volunteer Aid Association
Wedding invitations, reception lists, book list

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, sermons, pressed flowers, notebooks, clippings, ticket stubs, regional pamphlets, and Civil War clippings.

did.unittitle: Poems written by Jane L. Hardy
Poems written by Jane L. Hardy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes writings, notebooks, clippings, and sermons (particularly by William McHarg). Folder 5 contains a lock of "Uncle George's" hair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes financial statements, inspections, correspondence, state charity publications, reports, and speeches.

did.unittitle: Personal correspondence from relatives, mostly Bell Walker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes some correspondence to Louisa Hardy. Folder 10 contains correspondence regarding opinions on "Uncle Tom's Cabin".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Louisa Hardy's will, list of personal effects of Charles Hardy, and papers relating to the land in Kansas owned by Louisa Hardy.

did.unittitle: Letters from Charles and Louisa Hardy
Letters from Susan, George, Anna, and L. A. Walker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes in depth writings about crops planted at the plantation and the work done by enslaved peoples.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely created by Augusta Williams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included are two booklets published by Fleming Revell, and written by AHW, titled "Not Proven." and "How can I distinguish between Truth and Error in Religious Belief?". Also includes obituary clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Does not contain photographs, but a pamphlet from the Eastern Association listing Bess Williams in the nominating committee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summer house on Cayuga Lake.

scopecontent.p: Does not include photographs, but a wedding invitation, engraving, and obituary.

scopecontent.p: Includes clipping about fossils.

scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Utica house.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains obituary. Does not contain photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Francis Miles Finch and his granddaughter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of: Harriet N. Williams, Timothy Shaler Williams, Clara Wood, Rebecca Shaler, camp where George Williams served in the Civil War, Elias Hardy plaster cast, Sargeant John Clem, unidentified Williams children (possibly Henry, George, and Louisa Williams)

did.unittitle: Photographs of house at corner of Buffalo and Tioga Streets, Ithaca, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note reads "Where Grandpa C.E. Hardy lived when mother M.H.W. was a little girl."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a tribute written by her granddaughter Charlotte Williams.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Marion and Herbert H. Williams with sons Timothy and Herby
Group photographs of Josiah Williams' daughters
Photographs of Augusta Williams on her 100th birthday

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Mary Hardy Williams and other family members at Cliff Park.

did.unittitle: Family photographs and snapshots at Cliff Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter from Augusta to Clifford

did.unittitle: Robert Treman, wife, and baby "Bob" Treman
Photograph of a painted portrait of Martha Huggeford Hardy
Photographs of the interior of "Aunty's dining room, Hardy House, Clinton Street."
Photographs of lace from the Williams estate
Photographs of clothes and artifacts from Cliff Park

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Modelled and arranged by Mallett Sope Semonine.

did.unittitle: Framed portrait of Mary Hardy Williams
Framed portrait of Josiah B. Williams
Framed portrait of Henry Shaler Williams
Framed portrait painting by Louisa Walker Hardy
Framed portrait of Louise Hardy, Mary Hardy Williams, Henry Shaler Williams, and daughter
Portrait of Louisa Hardy in silver frame
